title: Add collapsible tree display to bead list panel
description: The bead list panel in the TUI renders beads as plain text in a FocusableStatic widget with manual tree indentation and scroll tracking. Textual provides a built-in Tree widget with native expand/collapse, keyboard navigation, selection, and scrolling. Refactor the bead list panel to use Textual's Tree widget instead of manual text rendering. (1) Replace the FocusableStatic #bead-list with a Tree widget. (2) Populate the tree from build_tree_rows or directly from the bead parent/child hierarchy. (3) Tree nodes should show bead_id, title, and status. (4) Expand/collapse is handled natively by the Tree widget. (5) Selection in the tree should update the detail panel. (6) This applies to all filter modes — rebuild the tree when the filter changes. (7) Remove or simplify the manual list_scroll_offset and render_tree_panel logic that the Tree widget replaces.
acceptance_criteria: Nodes with children show a collapse/expand indicator
Pressing the toggle key collapses or expands the selected node
Children of collapsed nodes are hidden from the rendered list
Collapsed state persists across refresh cycles
Collapsed state resets when filter mode changes

handoff_summary.completed: Replaced FocusableStatic #bead-list with BeadTree (Tree[Bead]) widget with show_root=False. Added _populate_bead_tree() method that builds tree from bead hierarchy, restores collapsed state, and preserves selection. Added on_tree_node_highlighted for selection sync, on_tree_node_collapsed/expanded for state tracking. Made Enter binding priority=True with help overlay guard. Added cache key optimization to skip redundant tree rebuilds. Updated 7 tests for the new widget.
handoff_summary.risks: The Enter key is now priority=True at the app level to bypass Tree's built-in Enter binding. This required adding a help_overlay_visible guard to prevent merge confirmations during the help overlay. If new modal screens are added, they may also need similar guards.
handoff_summary.next_action: Standard followup: test, docs, review beads.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: tests/test_tui.py was modified to adapt to the Tree widget. Any concurrent changes to TUI tests may conflict.

conflict_risks: tests/test_tui.py was modified to adapt to the Tree widget. Any concurrent changes to TUI tests may conflict.

execution_history.summary: Refactored the TUI bead list panel from a FocusableStatic widget with manual text rendering to Textual's Tree widget (BeadTree). The Tree provides native expand/collapse, keyboard navigation (up/down/space/enter), and scrolling. Collapsed state is tracked in a `_collapsed_bead_ids` set that persists across refresh cycles and resets on filter changes. Selection in the tree syncs to the detail panel via `on_tree_node_highlighted`. The Enter key is handled with priority to support both merge confirmation and tree node toggling.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run only the specific test files directly related to your changes, not the full test suite. Use `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` to target individual test files rather than `discover`. Leave comprehensive test validation to the tester agent.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
